executiveSummary.text: Strait of Hormuz reopened — oil crashes 10%+, inflation pressure easing but supply won't normalize for months
TSMC and ASML beat earnings but stocks fell — classic sell-the-news warning that AI semi expectations are at ceiling
MAS tightens monetary policy into SG Q1 GDP contraction (-0.3% QoQ) — banks benefit from NIM expansion, REITs hurt
SG banks sustaining 5-6% dividend yields with NIM declines reversing — income engine intact
Copper +9% MTD, gold +2% MTD — commodity supercycle thesis playing out, physical bottlenecks tightening
Samsung/SK Hynix win HBM4 slots — EWY (HBM proxy) thesis validated, Korean semi duopoly tightening grip
US tariff regime persists — 100% pharma tariffs, adjusted metals duties, $1.3T revenue over 2026-35
VIX at 17.48 — complacency risk. Geopolitical de-escalation may be premature, Iran situation still fluid

usGlobal.what: TSMC Q1 beat, guides capex to $52-56B high end — stock fell post-earnings
usGlobal.why: Sell-the-news on AI semis. The bar is so high that beating isn't enough. Equipment names (AMAT/KLAC/LRCX) also dipped despite bullish capex signal.

usGlobal.what: ASML lifts 2026 forecast to €36-40B revenue on surging AI chip demand — stock still sank
usGlobal.why: Fundamentals accelerating but valuation ceiling hit. 'Picks and shovels' thesis intact but entry price matters.

usGlobal.what: BofA hikes 2026 chips forecast to $1.3T — NVDA, AVGO, AMD named as top drivers
usGlobal.why: Revenue forecast raised $300B in 4 months. Capex wave is real but expectations may be running ahead of near-term execution.

usGlobal.what: Samsung & SK Hynix win Vera Rubin HBM4 supply slots, widening lead over Micron
usGlobal.why: HBM bottleneck between GPU and training throughput. Korean duopoly tightening. Micron losing HBM4 share specifically.

singapore.what: MAS tightens S$NEER policy band, raises inflation forecasts to 1.5-2.5%
singapore.why: SG Q1 GDP contracted -0.3% QoQ but MAS tightening on Iran-war inflation fears. SGD strengthening = good for purchasing power, bad for exporters.

singapore.what: SG bank dividends sustained — DBS 6.1% yield, OCBC/UOB ~5.4%
singapore.why: NIM declines reversing in Q4. OCBC outperforming DBS and UOB in 2026. Income engine intact and growing.

singapore.what: PhillipCapital replaces Singtel with Sembcorp in Absolute 10 portfolio
singapore.why: Sembcorp's Australian acquisition earnings-accretive. Energy transition capex thesis aligns with Sembcorp's pivot.

commodities.what: Strait of Hormuz reopened — oil crashes 10%+ in under 2 hours
commodities.why: Geopolitical de-escalation. Iran war premium deflating. But infrastructure damage means supply won't normalize for months — structural deficit persists.

commodities.what: Copper surges +9% MTD on supply constraints + AI data center demand
commodities.why: Copper is the irreplaceable physical bottleneck of AI buildout — wiring, transformers, grid. Structural demand story is 5+ years.

commodities.what: Sprott calls new commodity supercycle — copper, uranium, critical minerals in focus
commodities.why: Energy crisis + AI buildout + electrification = structural commodity demand. Nuclear policy shifting globally post-Iran war.

commodities.what: Gold holding above $4,700 — structural bull intact despite risk-on rotation
commodities.why: Weak DXY (98.20), persistent deficit concerns, and central bank buying support gold. Iran war de-escalation creates short-term headwind but structural floor remains.

narratives.title: Strait of Hormuz open — buy everything
narratives.description: Relief rally narrative spreading across WSB and Reddit. Oil crash interpreted as green light for risk assets. Short-covering, not new positioning.

narratives.title: AI capex is $700B+ and just getting started
narratives.description: Tech Twitter amplifying BofA/Jensen Huang capex numbers. Real but already priced 2-3 years forward. TSMC/ASML stock action confirms ceiling.
narratives.priceImpact: Bullish thesis but entry price matters

narratives.title: Nuclear is the energy story of 2026
narratives.description: Iran war made energy security a sovereign priority. Nuclear policy shifting globally. SMR and uranium stocks gaining institutional interest.
narratives.source: Seeking Alpha, Yahoo Finance, policy circles

narratives.title: Sell semi equipment, buy hyperscalers
narratives.description: Reddit narrative that equipment names are overbought after TSMC capex guidance. Dip is positioning washout, not fundamental change.
narratives.priceImpact: Likely wrong — capex guidance supports equipment

secondOrderEffects.text: MAS tightening + SGD strength = hidden drag on SG exporters and REITs. Your REIT position is small (2.79% of risk assets) so manageable, but watch refinancing costs closely.
secondOrderEffects.implication: REIT refinancing costs rise, but banks benefit from wider NIMs

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil crash = capex cuts for E&P, but AI data center buildout is energy-insensitive. Hyperscalers build regardless of oil prices because compute demand is non-negotiable. GRID and TSM/ASML benefit from this decoupling.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Energy transition and AI buildout are decoupling from oil prices

secondOrderEffects.text: Weak DXY (98.20) is a structural tailwind for gold and commodities. If dollar continues weakening on US deficit concerns and tariff disruption, GLD/COPX/URNM sleeve has support.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Commodity sleeve may benefit from prolonged USD weakness

secondOrderEffects.text: Trump pharma tariffs (100%) could reconfigure drug supply chains, indirectly supporting metals/commodities as industrial reshoring drives demand for physical materials.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Supply chain reconfiguration = infrastructure and metals demand

secondOrderEffects.text: SG Q1 GDP contraction (-0.3% QoQ) is a yellow flag. If Q2 doesn't recover, bank earnings could face credit quality questions. NIM expansion dominates for now, but monitor closely.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Bank dividends secure in near-term but watch credit quality

investment.thesis: Add to COPX on any pullback — copper is the irreplaceable physical bottleneck of the AI buildout
investment.whyNow: Copper +9% MTD but structural demand story (grid + data centers + EVs) is 5+ years. Your 3% allocation could support 4-5%.
investment.invalidator: Global recession kills commodity demand across the board

investment.thesis: Hold EWY with conviction — HBM4 supply slot wins confirm thesis
investment.whyNow: Samsung/SK Hynix securing Vera Rubin HBM4 slots. Korean market still cheap relative to semi exposure.
investment.invalidator: HBM demand collapse or Korean won destabilization

trading.thesis: If TSM bounces Mon-Tue from Friday's sell-the-news dip, consider a small add
trading.trigger: TSM holds support and bounces 3%+ from Friday close
trading.whyNow: TSMC at $52-56B capex with AI/HPC demand surging — fundamentals accelerating. The dip may be a gift.
trading.invalidator: Semi equipment continues bleeding into next week — would signal broader de-risking

watchlist.thesis: Monitor SG REITs for entry — S-REITs down 7% in March on SORA fears
watchlist.trigger: MAS pauses after this tightening (likely) and REITs stabilize
watchlist.whyNow: Your small position (2.79%) could be expanded to 3.5-4% at better prices. Worst may be priced in.
watchlist.invalidator: SORA continues rising sharply, crushing REIT financing economics

riskWatch.name: AI semi expectations reset
riskWatch.description: TSM/ASML 'sell the news' extends beyond post-earnings dip. If momentum doesn't recover by mid-week, signals broader semi de-risking.
riskWatch.mitigation: NVDA underweight is already defensive. Diversified across semi stack (equipment, EDA, foundry).

riskWatch.name: SG recession risk
riskWatch.description: Q2 GDP negative would raise bank credit quality questions. NIM expansion dominates now but watch NPL ratios in next earnings.
riskWatch.mitigation: Bank dividends secure. SG bank position is income, not growth.

riskWatch.name: Tariff escalation disrupts tech supply chains
riskWatch.description: 100% pharma tariffs and adjusted metals duties could escalate. TSMC Arizona fab partially hedges but semi equipment names more exposed.
riskWatch.mitigation: Equipment names are globally diversified. Watch for tariff exemptions on semiconductor equipment.

riskWatch.name: Oil re-spike if Hormuz closure reverses
riskWatch.description: Iran situation still fluid. If Hormuz closes again, oil spikes back to $120+. COPX/URNM/GRID actually benefit. GLD is a hedge.
riskWatch.mitigation: Commodity positions are thesis-driven hedges against exactly this scenario.

riskWatch.name: VIX complacency
riskWatch.description: VIX at 17.48 despite Iran war, tariff uncertainty, and GDP contraction signals. Market pricing perfection.
riskWatch.mitigation: Your 40.4% foundation (cash + CPF) provides genuine dry powder for any dislocation.

keyEvents.text: TSM/ASML post-earnings follow-through — buy the dip or continue selling?
Oil settlement after Hormuz reopening volatility — watch where Brent/WTI stabilize
Iran war escalation/reversal risk — any Hormuz closure reversal would spike oil
MAS policy digestion in STI and REIT pricing — watch for SORA direction this week
US tariff developments — pharma and metals tariff implementation details
Copper continuation — watch if +9% MTD extends or consolidates

bottomLine.whatMatters: Strait of Hormuz reopening is the biggest macro event. Oil crashing 10%+ is inflation-positive. But TSMC/ASML selling off despite beats is a warning — AI semi expectations at ceiling. MAS tightening into GDP contraction is uncomfortable but bank-positive.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold all positions. No panic, no FOMO. Watch TSM/semi equipment for a bounce Monday — potential dry powder deploy if the dip extends. Scarcity thesis (GLD, COPX, URNM, EWY) is playing out exactly as designed — let it compound. SG bank dividends secure and growing — collect and reinvest.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: WSB 'buy everything' Hormuz euphoria. 'AI capex is infinite' narrative — real but priced. Oil volatility — structural deficit persists but panic premium deflating. Short-term SG REIT noise — your position is small and the yield compensates.
